---
name: Finding report (a real magic constant in real code)
about: You ran exact on a published package and confirmed a genuine hand-typed constant
title: "[finding] "
labels: finding
---

**Package and version**
<!-- e.g. transformers 5.13.0, installed from PyPI -->

**Location**
<!-- file:line, and whether it's on the project's current main branch -->

**What exact reported**
```
<!-- paste the exact output -->
```

**Is it truncated or wrong?**
<!-- truncated = faithful but short; near-miss = a written digit is wrong -->

**Have you assessed impact?**
<!-- Honest take: does the precision loss actually matter here, or is it below
     the working precision of the computation? Both kinds of finding are
     welcome - we just want the assessment to be honest. -->

**Anything that would make this a "do not fix"?**
<!-- e.g. it's a faithful port of a reference algorithm, or a frozen standard
     revision. If you're not sure, say so. -->
